answer:
**Allama Iqbal's poem "Wataniyat" ("Nationalism") appears as Chapter 25 in *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*.**[^1] Here is the full English translation:

> [p-3b9de1] **Nationalism**  
> 
> [p-79b1e9] In this new age, the wine, the cup, the Jam are new,  
> The Saqi’s ways of grace and wrath are fashioned new.  
> The Muslim, too, has built his sanctum all anew,  
> While Civilization’s idol-smith carves idols new.  
> Of all these newer gods, the greatest is the Nation;  
> The robe it wears is Faith’s own final desecration.  
> 
> [p-2737d5] This idol, forged by this new age’s philosophy,  
> Lays waste the Prophet’s house of faith and piety.  
> Your arm is mighty with the strength of Oneness still,  
> Your homeland is Islam, you serve the Prophet’s will.  
> O Mustafavi, show the world that ancient sight!  
> And grind this hollow idol into dust and night!  
> 
> [p-a723b2] To be confined to one place is calamity,  
> Live free within the ocean, like a fish at sea.  
> To leave one’s home is the Beloved’s sacred way,  
> So bear you witness to the Prophet’s truth today.  
> In the discourse of the state, the Nation is but land;  
> In the Prophet’s teaching, Homeland is by God’s command.  
> 
> [p-97937b] Among the world’s great peoples, rivalry it sows,  
> The aim of trade becomes conquest where’er it goes.  
> From it, all politics from truth and candor flees,  
> And by its hand the weak man’s home is brought to its knees.  
> By it, God’s own creation is in pieces torn,  
> By it, the roots of Islam’s brotherhood are shorn.[^2]

Iqbal portrays territorial nationalism as a modern "idol" that desecrates Islamic faith, confines believers to mere land, fosters rivalry and conquest, and severs the universal brotherhood of Islam—urging Muslims to reject it in favor of a faith-centered "homeland."[^3][^4]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 25 (*Wataniyat*), [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/25-wataniyat/translation/).
[^2]: Ibid., [¶¶1-4](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/25-wataniyat/translation/#p-3b9de1).
[^3]: Ibid., [¶2](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/25-wataniyat/translation/#p-79b1e9).
[^4]: Ibid., [¶4](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/25-wataniyat/translation/#p-97937b).
